# Ubongo — Build Specification (v0.1: Multi-Agent + Self-Improving, CLI-First)

This document is the build spec for Ubongo v0.1. Phased releases with explicit acceptance criteria. v0.1 ships a working personal AI mind: a hand-rolled multi-agent runtime with continuous, GP-driven self-improvement, accessed through a local CLI. Telegram remains v0.2.

Build phase by phase. Each phase ships a system that's manually testable end-to-end. Don't move to phase N+1 until N's testing plan and smoke test pass.

## What Ubongo Is

A personal, mood-aware AI mind for one user (Giuseppe), running locally as a CLI. Inside, a **Master Agent** orchestrates a fleet of disposable **worker agents** (Research, Coding, Evaluator, Repair, Memory, Critic, Execution, Persona) across six execution modes (sequential, parallel, competitive, collaborative, debate, speculative). A **governance layer** evaluates risk, confidence, and reversibility per turn and gates risky actions through human approval. A **continuous self-improvement loop** uses Genetic Programming over prompts, routing rules, tool chains, and retry strategies — generations run autonomously, evaluation is sandboxed, promotions require user approval. Memory is SQLite-canonical with an Obsidian-compatible Markdown vault projection; embeddings (sqlite-vec) and graph relationships ride alongside.

The CLI is the v0.1 channel: REPL primary, one-shot for scripting. Telegram comes in v0.2.

## What Ubongo Is Not (v0.1)

- A multi-channel system. v0.1 is CLI-only. Telegram is deferred to v0.2; Slack, WhatsApp, Discord, web UI, voice are not on the roadmap.
- A production system or SaaS product.
- A multi-user or team tool.
- A distributed system. Single process, single machine.

If a feature is not explicitly listed in the phased build plan below, it is out of scope for v0.1.

## Core Design Decisions

1. **Single channel: CLI.** REPL plus one-shot. Telegram is v0.2.
2. **Master Agent orchestrates.** Not a router-then-LLM sequence; a real orchestrator that classifies, plans a workflow, dispatches workers, gates output, and composes the response.
3. **Worker agents are disposable.** Spawn per turn (or per workflow), execute, return results, dissolve. Durable intelligence lives in memory, workflows, policies, and the evolution lineage — not in agents.
4. **Hand-rolled orchestration.** Pure Python: classes, `asyncio` for parallel, an event bus for decoupling. No LangGraph, no Temporal, no Ray.
5. **Personas are voice; skills are capability; agents are role.** A Persona Agent wears a voice (Architect / Operator / Casual). A worker agent does a job (Research, Critic, etc.). A skill is a progressive-disclosure unit of capability that any agent can invoke.
6. **Hierarchical context.** System prompts are assembled per turn from `UBONGO.md` (global) + active persona + active skill body + worker-specific frame. Closer-to-task layers come last.
7. **SQLite canonical, Markdown projected, embeddings indexed, graph linked.** Conversation logs and structured facts live in SQLite. Daily-note Markdown projects out of SQLite for human readability. `sqlite-vec` indexes messages and vault notes for semantic recall. Vault-note links create a graph; the Master Agent can traverse it.
8. **Models via OpenRouter through LiteLLM.** Different models for different agents and decisions: small fast model for classification, strong model for architect, cheap model for casual, etc.
9. **All outbound messages flow through the queue.** Even synchronous CLI responses. The seam supports proactive output (v0.3) and Telegram delivery (v0.2) without restructuring.
10. **Governance is first-class.** Decision matrix evaluates Intent + Risk + Confidence + Context + Preferences + Reversibility per turn. High-risk actions gate through user approval.
11. **Self-improvement is continuous and approved.** GP loop runs in the background, generates and evaluates variants, persists lineage. Promotions to production require user approval via `/improvements`.
12. **Named events** are the extension surface. `before_classify`, `after_classify`, `before_plan`, `after_plan`, `before_execute`, `after_execute`, `before_govern`, `after_govern`, `before_compose`, `after_compose`, `before_send`, `after_send`, plus per-agent `agent_started`, `agent_completed`, `agent_failed`. Future behavior plugs in as event handlers.
13. **Configuration in YAML, secrets in `.env`.** Code reads config; config never contains secrets.
14. **Local-first, single-user.** No auth in CLI. When Telegram lands in v0.2, the `allowed_user_ids` allowlist comes back.

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology | Why |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Language | Python 3.11+ | Ecosystem, asyncio, fast iteration |
| LLM routing | LiteLLM | Provider abstraction without lock-in |
| Model provider | OpenRouter | Single key, every model, easy A/B |
| Storage | SQLite (stdlib) | Single-user, zero-ops |
| Vector index | `sqlite-vec` | Semantic recall over messages and vault, in the same DB |
| Config | YAML + Markdown | Editable without touching code |
| Secrets | python-dotenv | Standard `.env` loading |
| Tests | pytest | Standard |
| Package management | uv | Fast, modern |
| CLI parsing | stdlib argparse | No new dep |

No FastAPI, no Redis, no Docker, no LangGraph, no Temporal, no Ray, no Kubernetes. `python-telegram-bot` is added in v0.2 when the Telegram channel ships.

## Architecture

### The Core Loop

\```text
CLI input (REPL stdin or argv)
    |
    v   [event: before_classify]
Master Agent.classify(message, context)
    |   -> Classification(intent, tone, task_type, suggested_skill, risk, confidence)
    v   [event: after_classify]
Master Agent.plan(classification, context)
    |   -> Workflow(agents, execution_mode, persona, model_overrides)
    v   [event: before_execute]
Workflow Runner.execute(workflow, context)
    |   -> spawn agents per mode (sequential/parallel/etc.)
    |   -> route messages between agents
    |   -> aggregate results (Evaluator picks/merges)
    v   [event: after_execute]
Governance.gate(workflow_result, classification, context)
    |   -> Action(auto / ask_clarification / require_approval / reject)
    v   [event: before_compose]
Persona Agent.compose(workflow_result, persona, history)
    |   -> final user-facing text
    v   [event: after_compose]
enqueue(content, urgency='urgent', source='response')
    |
    v   [event: before_send]
dequeue + print to stdout
    |   [event: after_send]
    v
Memory Agent.write(turn) → SQLite + vault + embeddings
\```

For proactive messages (v0.3+) the flow starts at `enqueue`. The same workflow runner, governance gate, and persona composer apply. The GP loop runs in parallel, on its own asyncio task, hitting `OpenRouter` at low priority.

### The Master Agent

`src/ubongo/master.py`. Methods:

- `classify(message, context) -> Classification`. Single LLM call to the classifier model. JSON output with intent, tone, task_type, suggested_skill, risk, confidence. Falls back to default classification on parse failure.
- `plan(classification, context) -> Workflow`. Reads `routing.yaml` and `workflows.yaml`. Picks agents (which workers + which persona), execution mode, model overrides. Returns a `Workflow` object.
- `execute(workflow, context) -> WorkflowResult`. Delegates to the Workflow Runner.
- `decide(...) -> Decision`. Implements the decision matrix. v0.1 default: `auto` for everything until Phase 14 lands the rules.
- `handle(message) -> Response`. End-to-end orchestration: classify → plan → execute → governance → compose → enqueue → memory. This is what the REPL/oneshot calls.

### Worker Agents

Each implements the `Agent` protocol (`src/ubongo/agents/base.py`):

\```python
class Agent(Protocol):
    name: str
    role: str
    default_model: str

    async def run(self, input: AgentInput, context: Context) -> AgentResult: ...
\```

Workers are disposable: instantiated for a workflow, run once, return a `AgentResult`, dissolve. State that needs to persist goes through the Memory Agent.

| Worker | Purpose | v0.1 backend |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Research Agent | Retrieval + synthesis | LLM-only retrieval over conversation memory and vault. Web/calendar/email skills are v0.2+. |
| Coding Agent | Code generation, refactoring, review | Strong coding model (e.g., Claude Sonnet or equivalent). |
| Evaluator Agent | Validates output; produces confidence score | LLM-as-judge with criteria: correctness, completeness, hallucination signals. |
| Repair Agent | Detects and recovers failed workflows | Phase 13 logic: retry with different model, replace stuck agent, rollback. |
| Memory Agent | Single writer to durable memory | All persistence flows through here: messages, summaries, facts, vault, embeddings, lineage. |
| Critic Agent | Contrarian / brutal analysis | Argues against the prevailing answer. Used in debate mode and as a Master-Agent-summoned challenger when confidence is borderline. |
| Execution Agent | Runs shell scripts and external APIs | Constrained-bash skill in Phase 11; sandboxed in Phase 15. |
| Persona Agents | Voice for the user-facing surface | Architect / Operator / Casual; assemble the final response from worker output. |

### Execution Modes

`src/ubongo/runner.py` implements all six:

1. **Sequential.** A → B → C. Default for simple workflows.
2. **Parallel.** A | B | C, results aggregated by Evaluator. `asyncio.gather`.
3. **Competitive.** Same input, multiple agents, Evaluator picks the best output.
4. **Collaborative.** Each agent owns a subtask; results merged structurally (e.g., Research handles facts, Critic handles risks, both merged into a brief).
5. **Debate.** Two agents argue opposing positions for N rounds; Critic synthesizes the resolution.
6. **Speculative.** Cheap fast agent runs first; strong agent validates in the background. User sees the cheap result; if validation contradicts, the system corrects within the same session via a follow-up message.

Mode selection lives in `routing.yaml` and is overridable per workflow type. The GP loop can evolve the mode-selection rules.

### Governance Layer

`src/ubongo/governance/`.

- **Risk evaluation** (`risk.py`): per-workflow risk tag (`low`, `medium`, `high`, `destructive`). Initial rules read from `governance.yaml`.
- **Confidence** (`confidence.py`): from the Evaluator Agent's score. Threshold-based.
- **Reversibility** (`reversibility.py`): declared per skill in its frontmatter (`reversibility: reversible | irreversible`).
- **Decision matrix** (`decision.py`): given Intent, Risk, Confidence, Context, Preferences, Reversibility → Action ∈ {auto, ask_clarification, require_approval, reject}.
- **Approval** (`approval.py`): for `require_approval`, the CLI prompts the user with the proposed action and a one-line rationale; user types `yes` / `no` / `why` to confirm, reject, or expand.

### Self-Improvement (GP Loop)

`src/ubongo/evolution/`.

- **Generation** (`generator.py`): given a target (prompt / routing rule / tool chain / retry strategy), emit N variants. Variant strategies: paraphrase, prune, expand, recombine, perturb-temperature.
- **Sandbox evaluation** (`sandbox.py`): run a candidate against the held-out conversation sample. Sample is curated and anonymized from prior sessions. Each candidate produces a `EvaluationResult` (per-sample scores).
- **Fitness** (`fitness.py`): weighted sum of normalized success_rate, cost_inverse, latency_inverse, hallucination_inverse, user_correction_inverse. Weights live in `settings.yaml` and can themselves be evolved.
- **Selection + lineage** (`selection.py`, `lineage.py`): top variants survive. `evolution_lineage` table records parent → child edges, fitness scores, and timestamps.
- **Promotion** (`promotion.py`): top candidates are queued in `pending_promotions`; user approves via `/improvements`. Approved variants replace the live target; old version is preserved and reachable.
- **Loop** (`loop.py`): asyncio task runs at low priority, throttled by `evolution.max_calls_per_hour` in `settings.yaml`. Triggered manually with `/evolve <target>` or scheduled by `evolution.cron`.

\```

## Hierarchical Context Loader

`src/ubongo/context.py`. Function `build_system_prompt(persona_name, skill_name=None, agent_role=None) -> str`:

1. Read `config/UBONGO.md` body (cached).
2. Read `config/personas/<persona>.md` body (skipping frontmatter).
3. If `skill_name` is provided, read the skill body and prefix with `## Active Skill: <name>`.
4. If `agent_role` is provided (Worker Agent role like "Research" or "Critic"), append a short role-frame stanza from `agents/<role>.md` describing the agent's posture.
5. Concatenate with double newlines.

Caching: `UBONGO.md` and persona files at startup and on `/reload`. Skill bodies on demand, cached per skill, cleared on `/reload`. Worker role frames at startup.

## Named Events

`src/ubongo/events.py`. Synchronous-by-default dispatcher. v0.1 events:

| Event | Payload | Default handler |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `before_classify` | `{message, session}` | passthrough |
| `after_classify` | `{message, session, classification}` | passthrough |
| `before_plan` | `{classification, session}` | passthrough |
| `after_plan` | `{workflow}` | passthrough |
| `before_execute` | `{workflow, context}` | passthrough |
| `after_execute` | `{workflow_result}` | passthrough |
| `before_govern` | `{workflow_result, classification}` | passthrough |
| `after_govern` | `{decision, action}` | passthrough |
| `before_compose` | `{workflow_result, persona}` | passthrough |
| `after_compose` | `{response, persona}` | passthrough |
| `before_send` | `{queue_item, now}` | passthrough (policy engine in v0.2) |
| `after_send` | `{queue_item, output_id}` | vault projection, memory write |
| `agent_started` | `{agent, input}` | log |
| `agent_completed` | `{agent, result}` | log |
| `agent_failed` | `{agent, exception}` | log + repair trigger (Phase 13) |
| `evolution_generation` | `{target, generation, candidates}` | persist to lineage |
| `evolution_promotion` | `{target, candidate, fitness}` | queue for `/improvements` |

## Memory Model

SQLite schema (`memory/schema.sql`):

\```sql
-- conversations / messages / sessions / summaries / facts: as in v0 spec

CREATE TABLE conversations (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  started_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
  ended_at TIMESTAMP,
  active_persona TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE messages (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  conversation_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES conversations(id),
  role TEXT NOT NULL,
  content TEXT NOT NULL,
  timestamp TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
  persona TEXT,
  agent TEXT,
  skill TEXT,
  model TEXT,
  tokens_in INTEGER,
  tokens_out INTEGER
);

CREATE TABLE summaries (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  conversation_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
  covers_from_message_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
  covers_to_message_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
  content TEXT NOT NULL,
  strategy TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE sessions (
  user_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  last_message_at TIMESTAMP,
  active_persona TEXT,
  override_until TIMESTAMP,
  current_conversation_id INTEGER REFERENCES conversations(id)
);

CREATE TABLE facts (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  subject TEXT, predicate TEXT, object TEXT,
  source_message_id INTEGER REFERENCES messages(id),
  importance INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL
);

-- workflow execution trace

CREATE TABLE workflow_runs (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  conversation_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
  message_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
  classification JSON NOT NULL,
  workflow JSON NOT NULL,
  execution_mode TEXT NOT NULL,
  started_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
  ended_at TIMESTAMP,
  outcome TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (outcome IN ('success', 'failure', 'repaired'))
);

CREATE TABLE agent_runs (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  workflow_run_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES workflow_runs(id),
  agent TEXT NOT NULL,
  model TEXT,
  input JSON,
  output JSON,
  confidence REAL,
  tokens_in INTEGER,
  tokens_out INTEGER,
  latency_ms INTEGER,
  outcome TEXT NOT NULL,
  started_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
  ended_at TIMESTAMP
);

-- governance trace

CREATE TABLE governance_decisions (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  workflow_run_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES workflow_runs(id),
  intent TEXT, risk TEXT, confidence REAL,
  reversibility TEXT,
  action TEXT NOT NULL,
  approval_response TEXT,
  decided_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL
);

-- evolution

CREATE TABLE evolution_lineage (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  target TEXT NOT NULL,                -- e.g., 'persona:architect', 'rule:tech→architect'
  parent_id INTEGER REFERENCES evolution_lineage(id),
  generation INTEGER NOT NULL,
  variant_text TEXT NOT NULL,
  variant_metadata JSON,
  created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE evolution_evaluations (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  lineage_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES evolution_lineage(id),
  sample_set TEXT NOT NULL,
  success_rate REAL, cost REAL, latency_ms REAL,
  hallucination_rate REAL, user_correction_rate REAL,
  fitness REAL NOT NULL,
  evaluated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE pending_promotions (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  lineage_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES evolution_lineage(id),
  target TEXT NOT NULL,
  proposed_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
  decided_at TIMESTAMP,
  decision TEXT CHECK (decision IN ('approved', 'rejected'))
);

CREATE TABLE active_evolutions (
  target TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  lineage_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES evolution_lineage(id),
  promoted_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL
);

-- queue

CREATE TABLE notification_queue (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  content TEXT NOT NULL,
  urgency TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (urgency IN ('low', 'normal', 'urgent')),
  source TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
  deliver_after TIMESTAMP,
  delivered_at TIMESTAMP,
  expires_at TIMESTAMP,
  metadata JSON
);

-- vault graph (Phase 20)

CREATE TABLE vault_links (
  source_path TEXT NOT NULL,
  target_path TEXT NOT NULL,
  link_type TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (source_path, target_path, link_type)
);

-- vector index (Phase 20) lives in sqlite-vec virtual tables
-- vec_messages(embedding FLOAT[1536]) over messages.id
-- vec_vault(embedding FLOAT[1536]) over vault file paths

CREATE INDEX idx_messages_conversation ON messages(conversation_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_summaries_conversation ON summaries(conversation_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_queue_undelivered ON notification_queue(delivered_at) WHERE delivered_at IS NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_workflow_runs_conv ON workflow_runs(conversation_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_agent_runs_workflow ON agent_runs(workflow_run_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_lineage_target_gen ON evolution_lineage(target, generation);
CREATE INDEX idx_pending_undecided ON pending_promotions(decided_at) WHERE decided_at IS NULL;
\```

The Memory Agent is the only writer to durable memory. Other agents return their findings; Memory Agent commits.

## Tool Discipline

v0.1 exposes a small, curated tool surface to agents:

- **Constrained-bash skill** (Phase 11, sandboxed Phase 15): Execution Agent invokes shell scripts via this skill. Filesystem allowlist, env restriction, timeout.
- **Memory access**: indirect, through Memory Agent. No direct DB access from worker agents.
- **Vault read**: Research and Memory agents can read vault files.

When new capabilities arrive in v0.2+, prefer **CLI scripts invoked through the constrained-bash skill** over first-class tool definitions.

## Configuration Files

### `.env.example`

\```dotenv
# Required (v0.1)
OPENROUTER_API_KEY=

# Optional, for future phases (v0.2+)
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=
GOOGLE_CALENDAR_CLIENT_ID=
GOOGLE_CALENDAR_CLIENT_SECRET=
GMAIL_CLIENT_ID=
GMAIL_CLIENT_SECRET=
REDDIT_CLIENT_ID=
REDDIT_CLIENT_SECRET=
\```

### `config/settings.yaml`

\```yaml
models:
  classifier: openrouter/qwen/qwen-2.5-7b-instruct
  default: openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5
  casual: openrouter/anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5
  compaction: openrouter/anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5
  evaluator: openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5
  critic: openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5
  coding: openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5
  evolution_generator: openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5

api_keys:
  openrouter:
    env: OPENROUTER_API_KEY

memory:
  recall_turns: 10
  session_timeout_minutes: 30
  compaction:
    strategy: default
    trigger_at_turns: 30
  embeddings:
    enabled: true               # Phase 20 turns this on
    model: openrouter/openai/text-embedding-3-small
    recall_top_k: 5

vault:
  path: ./vault
  daily_notes_subdir: daily

governance:
  approval_required_on:
    - destructive
    - irreversible_high_risk
  confidence_threshold_for_auto: 0.7

evolution:
  enabled: true                 # Phase 18 turns this on
  max_calls_per_hour: 30
  population_size: 8
  generations_per_run: 3
  cron: null                    # set to a cron expression to schedule
  fitness_weights:
    success_rate: 0.40
    cost_inverse: 0.15
    latency_inverse: 0.10
    hallucination_inverse: 0.20
    user_correction_inverse: 0.15

logging:
  level: INFO
  format: json
\```

### `config/routing.yaml`

\```yaml
rules:
  - match: { intent: technical }
    workflow: technical_deep
  - match: { intent: work, task_type: command }
    workflow: quick_action
  - match: { intent: casual }
    workflow: casual_reply
  - match: { tone: frustrated }
    workflow: supportive_reply
  - match: { intent: research }
    workflow: research_brief
  - match: { intent: coding }
    workflow: coding_session
  - match: { task_type: high_stakes_decision }
    workflow: debate_then_synthesize
default_workflow: casual_reply
\```

### `config/workflows.yaml`

\```yaml
workflows:
  technical_deep:
    persona: architect
    agents: [research, evaluator, persona]
    mode: sequential
    risk: low
  quick_action:
    persona: operator
    agents: [persona]
    mode: sequential
    risk: low
  casual_reply:
    persona: casual
    agents: [persona]
    mode: sequential
    risk: low
  supportive_reply:
    persona: casual
    agents: [persona]
    mode: sequential
    risk: low
  research_brief:
    persona: architect
    agents: [research, critic, evaluator, persona]
    mode: collaborative
    risk: low
  coding_session:
    persona: architect
    agents: [coding, evaluator, persona]
    mode: sequential
    risk: medium
  debate_then_synthesize:
    persona: architect
    agents: [research, critic, evaluator, persona]
    mode: debate
    risk: medium
  speculative_brief:
    persona: operator
    agents: [research_cheap, research_strong, evaluator, persona]
    mode: speculative
    risk: low
\```

### `config/governance.yaml`

\```yaml
risk_rules:
  - skill: constrained-bash
    risk: medium
  - intent: notification_control
    risk: low
  - tool: any_external_write
    risk: high
  - destructive_keywords: ["delete", "drop", "rm -rf", "force push"]
    risk: destructive

decision_thresholds:
  auto_max_risk: low
  approval_min_risk: high
  reject_below_confidence: 0.2
\```

## CLAUDE.md (for future Claude Code sessions)

The project ships [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md) at the root. It contains the project description, what's in scope and out, current phase status (pointing at [STATUS.md](STATUS.md)), conventions (prose over bullets, no em-dashes, no emojis, direct tone), and architectural rules (Master Agent orchestrates, every outbound message through the queue, secrets only in `.env`, new behavior as event handlers, new capabilities default to CLI scripts invoked via the constrained-bash skill).

---

## Phased Build Plan

22 phases organized into 6 tiers. Each phase ends with a working, end-to-end-testable system. The cumulative manual playbook lives at `tests/manual/smoke_test.md` and grows as phases land.

### Tier 1 — Foundation (Phases 0–7)

These phases reach a working single-agent CLI that classifies, routes, persists, projects to vault, runs skills, and queues all output. By end of Tier 1, you have the v0.1 of the *previous* spec, working — that becomes the substrate for the multi-agent runtime in Tier 2.

### Phase 0 — Skeleton

**Goal:** A `uv run python -m ubongo` invocation that loads config, sets up structured logging, and exits cleanly. Pure scaffolding to prove the harness.

**Sub-phases:**

- **0a — Project init.** `uv init`; `pyproject.toml` with deps (`litellm`, `python-dotenv`, `pyyaml`, `pytest`, `sqlite-vec`); `uv sync` works.
- **0b — Config loading.** `config.py` reads `settings.yaml`, resolves env-var refs, validates required fields, raises on missing key.
- **0c — Hierarchical context loader.** `context.py` provides `build_system_prompt(persona, skill=None, agent_role=None)`.
- **0d — Structured JSON logging.** `logging.py`; configurable level; one startup log line with config summary (no secrets).
- **0e — CLI entry.** `__main__.py` with argparse; default action prints startup line; `send` subcommand parsed but no-op for now.

**Files touched:** `pyproject.toml`, `src/ubongo/__init__.py`, `src/ubongo/__main__.py`, `src/ubongo/config.py`, `src/ubongo/context.py`, `src/ubongo/logging.py`, `config/settings.yaml`, `config/UBONGO.md`, `config/personas/{architect,operator,casual}.md`, `.env.example`.

**Testing plan:**

| # | Scenario | Steps | Expected |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Cold start | `uv run python -m ubongo` | JSON startup line; rc 0. |
| 2 | Missing API key | unset `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`; run | rc 1; clear error pointing at the missing var; no traceback dump. |
| 3 | Context assembly | `python -c "from ubongo.context import build_system_prompt; print(build_system_prompt('architect'))"` | UBONGO.md body, blank line, architect.md body. |
| 4 | Log structure | Capture stderr; pipe to `jq .` | Valid JSON. Has `event`, `level`, `ts`. No secrets. |

**End-to-end manual smoke test:** N/A for Phase 0 (no user-facing surface yet). The smoke playbook starts in Phase 1.

**Acceptance:** all 4 scenarios pass; nothing else under `src/ubongo/` exists yet.

### Phase 1 — CLI REPL + One-Shot (echo mode)

**Goal:** REPL accepts input and echoes back with current persona name. One-shot mode runs a single turn and exits. Slash commands switch personas. No LLM yet.

**Sub-phases:**

- **1a — REPL loop.** `repl.py`: prompt `> `; read line; dispatch.
- **1b — One-shot command.** `oneshot.py`: parse `send "<msg>" [--persona <name>]`; run one turn; exit.
- **1c — Slash command parser.** `/architect`, `/operator`, `/casual`, `/auto`, `/exit`. Active persona stored in-process.
- **1d — Echo response.** Output `[<persona>] <input>` for any text turn.
- **1e — `__main__.py` dispatch.** No args → REPL; `send <msg>` → one-shot.

**Files touched:** `src/ubongo/repl.py`, `src/ubongo/oneshot.py`, `src/ubongo/__main__.py`.

**Testing plan:**

| # | Scenario | Steps | Expected |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | REPL echo | Start REPL; type "hello" | `[architect] hello` |
| 2 | Persona switch | `/casual`; type "hello" | `[casual] hello` |
| 3 | `/auto` | After `/casual`, type `/auto`; type "hello" | `[architect] hello` (auto defaults to architect for now) |
| 4 | One-shot | `python -m ubongo send "hello" --persona operator` | Prints `[operator] hello`; exits 0. |
| 5 | `/exit` | Type `/exit` | REPL exits cleanly; rc 0. |

**End-to-end manual smoke test (initialize `tests/manual/smoke_test.md`):**

1. `python -m ubongo`. Confirm startup log + REPL prompt.
2. Try `/architect`, `/operator`, `/casual` and a message after each. Confirm bracket label changes.
3. `/exit`.
4. `python -m ubongo send "hi" --persona casual`. Confirm `[casual] hi` then exit.

**Acceptance:** all 5 scenarios pass; `tests/manual/smoke_test.md` created with the 4 steps above as "Phase 1" section.

### Phase 2 — LLM Integration

**Goal:** Real responses through LiteLLM/OpenRouter using hierarchical prompts. Personas now feel different.

**Sub-phases:**

- **2a — Persona registry.** `agents/personas.py`: load each persona file (frontmatter + body) at startup. Frontmatter declares `default_model`, `max_tokens`.
- **2b — LiteLLM wrapper.** `llm.py`: `complete(system_prompt, messages, model, max_tokens) -> CompletionResult`. Single retry with exponential backoff on transient errors.
- **2c — Wire into REPL/oneshot.** Replace echo: build system prompt via `build_system_prompt(persona)`, call LLM, return text.
- **2d — Event scaffolding.** Emit `before_llm` and `after_llm` (passthrough handlers) around the LLM call. Adds a seam for Phase 8.
- **2e — Error path.** On terminal LLM error: short polite message, log the cause.

**Files touched:** `src/ubongo/agents/personas.py`, `src/ubongo/llm.py`, `src/ubongo/repl.py`, `src/ubongo/oneshot.py`, `src/ubongo/events.py`.

**Testing plan:**

| # | Scenario | Steps | Expected |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Architect mode | "design a circuit breaker for an API gateway" with `/architect` | Substantive technical response with tradeoff discussion. |
| 2 | Casual mode | "ugh today sucked" with `/casual` | Short, warm reply. |
| 3 | Operator mode | "summarize my last 3 commits" with `/operator` | Terse, action-oriented response (LLM may caveat about not having git access — acceptable). |
| 4 | UBONGO.md effect | Edit `config/UBONGO.md` to add a quirky preference; restart; ask any question | Response respects the new preference. |
| 5 | LLM error | Set bogus API key; run | Polite error message; no traceback to stdout. |

**Smoke test additions (Phase 2 section in `smoke_test.md`):**

1. Architect / Operator / Casual each respond and feel different.
2. Persona switch mid-conversation reflects in next response.
3. One-shot: `python -m ubongo send "what's 7*8" --persona operator` returns "56" or terse equivalent.

**Acceptance:** scenarios pass; smoke test passes; `STATUS.md` Phase 2 row marked complete.

### Phase 3 — Tone Classifier + Auto Routing

**Goal:** In `/auto` mode, the system classifies each message and picks the persona automatically. Slash overrides still work.

**Sub-phases:**

- **3a — Classifier function.** `classifier.py`: single LLM call to the small classifier model; JSON output `{intent, tone, task_type, suggested_skill, risk, confidence}`. Defensive parsing.
- **3b — Routing logic.** `router.py`: load `routing.yaml`; apply rules; return persona. (Becomes a private helper of Master Agent in Phase 8.)
- **3c — Hysteresis.** Within a session, only switch persona on confidence > 0.7 AND a new persona suggestion.
- **3d — Wire `before_classify` / `after_classify` events** (passthroughs).
- **3e — Per-turn classification log.** Log each message's classification + chosen persona.

**Files touched:** `src/ubongo/classifier.py`, `src/ubongo/router.py`, `config/routing.yaml`, `src/ubongo/repl.py`, `src/ubongo/oneshot.py`.

**Testing plan:**

| # | Scenario | Steps | Expected |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Auto-route to architect | `/auto`; "help me design a circuit breaker" | Classifier returns intent=technical; architect persona; response is architect-style. |
| 2 | Auto-route to casual | `/auto`; "ugh long day" | Classifier returns intent=casual or tone=frustrated; casual persona. |
| 3 | Hysteresis | Five technical messages, then one offhand "lol" | Persona stays architect (low confidence on the offhand). |
| 4 | Manual override beats auto | `/auto`; ask technical question; `/casual` for next | Next response in casual voice, despite intent=technical. |
| 5 | Classifier failure | Force JSON parse error in test mode | Falls back to default persona; logs the failure. |

**Smoke test additions:**

1. Auto mode picks reasonable personas across a 5-message mixed conversation.
2. `/architect` mid-session sticks until `/auto`.

**Acceptance:** scenarios pass; smoke test passes; `STATUS.md` updated.

### Phase 4 — SQLite Memory + Compaction

**Goal:** Conversations persist across restarts. Recall returns recent turns + a compaction summary for older history.

**Sub-phases:**

- **4a — Schema + migrations.** `memory/schema.sql`; on-startup `CREATE IF NOT EXISTS` for all tables defined above. (Tables for workflows/agents/governance/evolution exist as empty tables ahead of their respective phases.)
- **4b — Store API.** `memory/store.py`: start/get/end conversations; append messages; get session state; get last N messages; persist summaries.
- **4c — Session definition.** Same user, last_message_at gap < 30 minutes. Updated on every message.
- **4d — Compaction.** `memory/compaction.py`: registry pattern; default impl summarizes older messages into one paragraph using the compaction model. Triggered when total turns > `compaction.trigger_at_turns`. Persists summary; recall combines summary + last N.
- **4e — Wire `after_recall` event.** Compaction handler attaches here.
- **4f — Wire `after_llm` event.** Memory write handler attaches here.
- **4g — Move active persona / override into `sessions` table.**

**Files touched:** `src/ubongo/memory/schema.sql`, `src/ubongo/memory/store.py`, `src/ubongo/memory/compaction.py`, `src/ubongo/repl.py`, `src/ubongo/oneshot.py`, `src/ubongo/events.py`.

**Testing plan:**

| # | Scenario | Steps | Expected |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Persistence | 5-turn conversation; `/exit`; restart REPL within 30 min | Bot remembers the topic. |
| 2 | New session | After 31 min silence, send a message | New `conversations` row; old context not in recall. |
| 3 | Compaction trigger | Reach 31 turns | Summary row persisted; subsequent recall returns summary + last 10 turns. |
| 4 | Compaction idempotency | Continue past 31 turns; send 5 more | Existing summary not re-generated; new turns not re-summarized until next trigger. |
| 5 | Swappable strategy | Register a stub compaction returning `"STUB"`; trigger | Recall uses `"STUB"`. |

**Smoke test additions:**

1. Restart-resumes-conversation works.
2. After ~30 turns, you can ask "what have we been talking about" and get a coherent summary.

**Acceptance:** scenarios pass; smoke passes; `STATUS.md` updated.

### Phase 5 — Markdown Vault Projection

**Goal:** Daily notes generated in Obsidian-compatible Markdown. Read-only in v0.1 (sync is Phase 21).

**Sub-phases:**

- **5a — Vault writer.** `memory/vault.py`: `append_to_daily_note(date, user_message, response, persona)`. Format: chronological with timestamp + persona header.
- **5b — Default `after_send` handler.** Calls vault writer.
- **5c — Vault structure.** `vault/daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md`. Initial directories created lazily.
- **5d — Obsidian-compatibility check.** Wikilinks, frontmatter, headings render correctly.

**Files touched:** `src/ubongo/memory/vault.py`, `src/ubongo/events.py`, `vault/.gitkeep`.

**Testing plan:**

| # | Scenario | Steps | Expected |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Daily note write | Send 3 messages | `vault/daily/<today>.md` exists; 3 entries with timestamps + persona headers. |
| 2 | Obsidian rendering | Open `vault/` as Obsidian vault | Daily note renders cleanly; no broken syntax. |
| 3 | Handler disable | Unregister vault handler at runtime; send a message | No new vault write; SQLite still updated. |
| 4 | Date rollover | Mock time to advance to next day; send a message | New `<tomorrow>.md` file created. |

**Smoke test additions:**

1. After a few messages, verify today's vault note exists with the right entries.

**Acceptance:** scenarios pass; smoke passes.

### Phase 6 — Skills + Progressive Disclosure

**Goal:** Skills as folders with frontmatter + body. Descriptions load at startup; bodies on activation. v0.1 ships `summarize-conversation`.

**Sub-phases:**

- **6a — Skill discovery.** `skills.py`: scan `config/skills/`; parse `SKILL.md` frontmatter only; build a registry by name with descriptions.
- **6b — Lazy body loading.** Body read from disk and cached on first activation. `/reload` clears the cache.
- **6c — Classifier skill suggestion.** Pass list of skill names + descriptions in classifier prompt. Expect optional `suggested_skill` in JSON output.
- **6d — Skill resolution order.** Explicit slash command (e.g., `/summary`) > classifier suggestion > manual `/skill <name>` for next message.
- **6e — `summarize-conversation` skill.** Triggers on `/summary`. Operator persona; 3-5 sentence summary of the current session.
- **6f — `/skills` and `/reload` REPL commands.**

**Files touched:** `src/ubongo/skills.py`, `config/skills/summarize-conversation/SKILL.md`, `config/skills/summarize-conversation/prompts/summarize.md`, `src/ubongo/repl.py`, `src/ubongo/classifier.py`.

**Testing plan:**

| # | Scenario | Steps | Expected |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | `/summary` works | After 5-turn conversation, type `/summary` | Coherent 3-5 sentence summary in operator voice. |
| 2 | Skill catalog | `/skills` | Lists `summarize-conversation` with description. |
| 3 | `/reload` picks up edits | Edit skill body; `/reload`; trigger again | New body in effect. |
| 4 | Body lazy-load | Inspect logs at startup vs after `/summary` | Skill body not loaded at startup; loaded on first `/summary`. |
| 5 | Classifier suggestion | "can you wrap this up for me" | Classifier suggests `summarize-conversation`; runs the skill. |

**Smoke test additions:**

1. `/summary` produces a sensible summary.
2. `/skills` lists what's available.
3. `/reload` after editing a persona file reflects on next message.

**Acceptance:** scenarios pass; smoke passes.

### Phase 7 — Minimal Outbound Queue

**Goal:** Every CLI response flows through the SQLite-backed queue. Seam for Telegram (v0.2) and proactive output (v0.3) is in place.

**Sub-phases:**

- **7a — Queue API.** `delivery/queue.py`: `enqueue(content, urgency, source, ...)`, `dequeue_deliverable(now)`, `mark_delivered(id, now)`.
- **7b — Refactor response path.** Enqueue at `urgency=urgent`, source `response`; immediately dequeue + fire `before_send` (passthrough) + print + fire `after_send` (vault) + mark delivered.
- **7c — `/queue` REPL command.** Print last N rows from `notification_queue` (delivered + undelivered).

**Files touched:** `src/ubongo/delivery/queue.py`, `src/ubongo/repl.py`, `src/ubongo/oneshot.py`, `src/ubongo/events.py`.

**Testing plan:**

| # | Scenario | Steps | Expected |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Queue contains response | Send a message; `SELECT * FROM notification_queue` | Row exists with `delivered_at` set. |
| 2 | `/queue` command | Send 3 messages; `/queue` | 3-row table with timestamps and urgencies. |
| 3 | Latency | Time the round-trip | No perceptible delay vs Phase 6 baseline. |
| 4 | Event hooks | Register a no-op handler on `before_send`; send a message | Response still delivered; handler runs. |
| 5 | Vault still works | After a response, check `vault/daily/<today>.md` | Entry present (after_send vault handler still firing). |

**Smoke test additions:**

1. `/queue` shows a non-empty table after a few turns.
2. Cumulative test: full Phase 1–7 walkthrough still passes.

**Acceptance:** scenarios pass; smoke passes; **end of Tier 1**.

---

### Tier 2 — Multi-Agent System (Phases 8–12)

These phases install the Master Agent over the existing flow, then introduce the worker fleet, then the six execution modes.

### Phase 8 — Master Agent

**Goal:** The Master Agent wraps the existing single-agent flow without changing user-visible behavior. Establishes the seam workers will plug into.

**Sub-phases:**

- **8a — `MasterAgent` class.** `master.py`: `classify`, `plan`, `execute`, `decide`, `handle`. In Phase 8, `plan` always returns a one-agent workflow with the chosen persona.
- **8b — Decision matrix scaffold.** `governance/decision.py`: `Decision = (Intent, Risk, Confidence, Context, Preferences, Reversibility) -> Action`. Returns `auto` for everything in Phase 8.
- **8c — Migrate response path.** REPL/oneshot calls `MasterAgent.handle(message)`. Internally: classify → plan → execute → governance → compose. Single-agent execute reuses Phase 2 persona path.
- **8d — Logging.** Emit `master_decision` log with classification + workflow + decision per turn.
- **8e — `/decisions` REPL command.** Last N `master_decision` entries from current session as a table.

**Files touched:** `src/ubongo/master.py`, `src/ubongo/governance/__init__.py`, `src/ubongo/governance/decision.py`, `src/ubongo/repl.py`, `src/ubongo/oneshot.py`, `src/ubongo/router.py`.

**Testing plan:**

| # | Scenario | Steps | Expected |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Behavior parity | Same prompts as Phase 7 baseline | Same responses (modulo model nondeterminism). |
| 2 | Decision logged | "design a circuit breaker" | `master_decision` log line: `intent=technical persona=architect mode=sequential risk=low`. |
| 3 | `/decisions` | Send 3; `/decisions` | 3-row table. |
| 4 | High-risk passthrough | Force a classification with `risk=high` | Decision returns `auto` (rules ship Phase 14). |
| 5 | Classifier crash | Inject classifier exception | Falls back to default persona; logs error; response produced. |

**Smoke test additions:**

1. `/decisions` populated after a few turns.
2. Cumulative Phase 1–8 walkthrough still passes.

**Acceptance:** scenarios pass; smoke passes.

### Phase 9 — First Workers (Research + Memory)

**Goal:** Real worker agents enter the system. Master Agent picks Research for research-y intents; Memory Agent becomes the single writer to durable memory.

**Sub-phases:**

- **9a — Agent base.** `agents/base.py`: `Agent` protocol; `AgentInput`, `AgentResult` dataclasses; `agent_started` / `agent_completed` / `agent_failed` events.
- **9b — Research Agent.** `agents/research.py`: prompt frame "you are a Research Agent…"; takes a question + optional context; returns synthesis with cited prior turns. v0.1 backend: LLM-only over conversation memory + vault snippets fetched by Memory Agent.
- **9c — Memory Agent.** `agents/memory.py`: single-writer for `messages`, `summaries`, `facts`, vault, embeddings. Migrate the Phase 4 `after_llm` memory-write handler to call Memory Agent instead of writing directly.
- **9d — Workflow runner skeleton.** `runner.py`: sequential mode only. `execute(workflow) -> WorkflowResult` runs agents in order, threading each agent's `AgentResult` into the next agent's `AgentInput.context`.
- **9e — Master Agent picks Research.** `routing.yaml` `research_brief` workflow now resolves to `[research, persona]` agents; Master Agent dispatches via runner.
- **9f — `/agents` REPL command.** Lists registered agents with role + default model.
- **9g — `workflow_runs` and `agent_runs` writes.** Memory Agent persists each turn's workflow trace.

**Files touched:** `src/ubongo/agents/base.py`, `src/ubongo/agents/research.py`, `src/ubongo/agents/memory.py`, `src/ubongo/runner.py`, `src/ubongo/master.py`, `config/workflows.yaml`, `src/ubongo/repl.py`.

**Testing plan:**

| # | Scenario | Steps | Expected |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Research dispatched | "research what we discussed about caching last week" | Research Agent ran (visible in `agent_runs` table); response cites prior turns. |
| 2 | `/agents` | `/agents` | Lists `research`, `memory`, `architect`, `operator`, `casual` (Phase 1 personas formalized as agents in Phase 10 — for now Memory and Research at minimum). |
| 3 | Workflow trace | After a research turn, `SELECT * FROM workflow_runs WHERE id = (SELECT max(id) FROM workflow_runs)` | Row with execution_mode=sequential; corresponding agent_runs rows. |
| 4 | Memory single-writer | Trigger memory write from a non-Memory agent (test mode) | Raises; no DB write outside Memory Agent. |
| 5 | Casual still works | `/casual`; "long day" | Single-agent (persona only) workflow; response in casual voice. |

**Smoke test additions:**

1. A research-style question shows a Research Agent run in `/agents` traces (we'll add a `/trace` cmd in Phase 10).
2. Cumulative walkthrough still passes.

**Acceptance:** scenarios pass; smoke passes.

### Phase 10 — Evaluator + Critic + Persona Agents Formalized

**Goal:** Three more workers; persona switching now goes through Persona Agents; Evaluator produces a confidence number used by governance.

**Sub-phases:**

- **10a — Evaluator Agent.** `agents/evaluator.py`: LLM-as-judge. Input: a workflow result. Output: confidence score (0-1) + brief justification + flagged issues (hallucination, missing context).
- **10b — Critic Agent.** `agents/critic.py`: contrarian frame. Input: a proposal. Output: counterargument + risks. Used by Master Agent for borderline-confidence turns and by debate mode (Phase 12).
- **10c — Persona Agents.** `agents/personas.py` becomes class-based: `PersonaAgent(Agent)` subclassed by `ArchitectPersona`, `OperatorPersona`, `CasualPersona`. The Phase 2 voice-switching flow now goes through these classes.
- **10d — Master Agent uses Evaluator.** After workflow execute and before governance, call Evaluator. Confidence flows into the decision.
- **10e — `/trace <n>` REPL command.** Print full workflow + agent run trace for the n-th most recent turn.

**Files touched:** `src/ubongo/agents/evaluator.py`, `src/ubongo/agents/critic.py`, `src/ubongo/agents/personas.py`, `src/ubongo/master.py`, `src/ubongo/governance/decision.py`, `src/ubongo/repl.py`.

**Testing plan:**

### Phase 11 — Coding + Execution + Repair Agents

**Goal:** The remaining workers ship. Execution Agent runs shell scripts via the constrained-bash skill (sandboxing comes Phase 15). Repair Agent registered with single-retry behavior.

**Sub-phases:**

- **11a — Coding Agent.** `agents/coding.py`: code-focused frame; uses the strong coding model; returns annotated code with explanation.
- **11b — Constrained-bash skill.** `config/skills/constrained-bash/`: SKILL.md with frontmatter declaring `risk: medium`, `reversibility: irreversible`. Body explains the contract: read-only filesystem allowlist (default: project subdir + `/tmp`), no network, 10s timeout. v0.1 enforcement: subprocess + restricted PATH; full sandbox in Phase 15.
- **11c — Execution Agent.** `agents/execution.py`: invokes constrained-bash; produces stdout/stderr/exit-code result. Activates on `coding_session` workflow when the user asks to actually run something; otherwise the Coding Agent just returns code.
- **11d — Repair Agent (registered).** `agents/repair.py`: single-retry on agent failure with a different model from `settings.yaml` retry table. Activated by `agent_failed` event handler. (Multi-step recovery is Phase 13.)
- **11e — `/exec <cmd>` REPL command** for direct constrained-bash invocation (debug only; not user-facing).
- **11f — Workflow `coding_session` lit up.** Routes coding-related intents to `[coding, evaluator, persona]`; with execution intent (e.g., "run this"), adds `execution`.

### Phase 12 — Execution Modes (all six)

**Goal:** Workflow Runner supports sequential, parallel, competitive, collaborative, debate, speculative. Master Agent picks mode per workflow.

**Sub-phases:**

- **12a — Parallel mode.** `runner.py`: `asyncio.gather` over independent agents; Evaluator aggregates.
- **12b — Competitive mode.** Same input to multiple agents; Evaluator picks the best (tagged with reasoning).
- **12c — Collaborative mode.** Workflow declares per-agent subtask via `agent_role` field; results merged structurally.
- **12d — Debate mode.** Two agents (typically Research + Critic) argue; N rounds (default 2); Evaluator synthesizes.
- **12e — Speculative mode.** Cheap agent runs first, returns to user; strong agent validates in background; if mismatch, system sends a follow-up correction (`Master Agent.followup` + queue).
- **12f — Mode selection.** `workflows.yaml` declares `mode` per workflow; Master Agent honors it.
- **12g — `/mode <workflow>` debug command.** Force a specific workflow (and thus mode) for the next message; useful for testing.

**Files touched:** `src/ubongo/runner.py`, `src/ubongo/master.py`, `src/ubongo/agents/evaluator.py` (aggregation logic), `config/workflows.yaml`, `src/ubongo/repl.py`.

**Testing plan:**

| # | Scenario | Steps | Expected |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Sequential | Standard architect question | Same as Phase 10. |
| 2 | Parallel | `/mode research_brief`; "compare Postgres vs DynamoDB for this use case" | Research and Critic run concurrently; Evaluator merges. Total latency < sequential. |
| 3 | Competitive | Configure a `coding_competitive` test workflow with two coding models; ask coding question | Both run; Evaluator picks winner with reasoning. |
| 4 | Collaborative | `research_brief` with `mode=collaborative` | Research handles facts; Critic handles risks; merged into structured brief. |
| 5 | Debate | `/mode debate_then_synthesize`; "should we use microservices for this 5-engineer team" | Research and Critic argue 2 rounds; Evaluator synthesizes "no" with caveats. |
| 6 | Speculative | `/mode speculative_brief`; ask a quick factual question | Cheap response shows immediately; if strong validation contradicts, follow-up correction in same session within ~10s. |

**Smoke test additions:**

1. Run each of the six modes via `/mode` once; confirm distinguishable behavior.

**Acceptance:** scenarios pass; smoke passes; **end of Tier 2**.

---

### Tier 3 — Self-Healing (Phase 13)

### Phase 13 — Repair Agent Activated

**Goal:** Real failure detection + multi-step recovery.

**Sub-phases:**

- **13a — Failure taxonomy.** `agents/repair.py`: distinguishes timeout, model error, parse error, content rejection, infinite loop. Each maps to a recovery strategy.
- **13b — Multi-strategy retry.** Retry with: same model + different prompt frame; different model same prompt; smaller model with shorter prompt; abort + apologize.
- **13c — Agent replacement.** If a worker fails on a workflow, swap in a fallback agent (declared per worker in `settings.yaml`).
- **13d — Workflow rollback.** If a multi-agent workflow fails partway, roll back any side effects (notably Memory Agent writes that aren't yet committed). Use a write-buffer pattern: agents queue writes; Memory Agent commits on success, drops on failure.
- **13e — Repair audit.** Each repair logged with `repair_run` rows linked to the original `workflow_run`.
- **13f — User-visible behavior.** On unrecoverable failure: a clear apology message naming the problem ("the coding model didn't respond — fall back to architect?") and a y/n.

**Files touched:** `src/ubongo/agents/repair.py`, `src/ubongo/runner.py`, `src/ubongo/agents/memory.py` (write-buffer), `src/ubongo/master.py`, `src/ubongo/memory/schema.sql` (add `repair_runs` table).

**Testing plan:**

| # | Scenario | Steps | Expected |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Timeout recovery | Inject 30s timeout in coding model | Repair retries with smaller model; user gets a response within ~15s. |
| 2 | Parse error recovery | Inject malformed JSON from classifier | Repair re-prompts with stricter schema; classification succeeds. |
| 3 | Agent replacement | Disable Coding Agent | Master Agent uses architect persona for code questions; logs the substitution. |
| 4 | Rollback | Inject a failure mid-collaborative workflow | No partial messages persisted; vault note unaffected; user sees a clean error. |
| 5 | Unrecoverable | Inject persistent failure across all retry strategies | User sees the apology + y/n; "n" returns the user to a clean prompt. |

**Smoke test additions:**

1. Manually trigger a failure scenario (e.g., bogus model name); confirm graceful recovery.

**Acceptance:** scenarios pass; smoke passes.

- **15b — Approval persisted.** Every approval/rejection captured in `governance_decisions.approval_response`.
- **15c — Execution Agent sandbox.** Subprocess with: empty PATH, restricted env (only allowlisted vars), CWD = project subdir, filesystem read/write allowlist enforced via path validation, no network (no `requests`, `urllib`; subprocess invoked with controlled env only), 10s wallclock timeout.
- **15d — Sandbox tests.** Negative tests: out-of-allowlist read fails; network call fails; long-running command times out.

### Phase 17 — Sandboxed Evaluation + Fitness

**Goal:** Variants are evaluated against the held-out conversation sample; fitness score computed.

**Sub-phases:**

- **17d — `evolution_evaluations` writes.** Per-variant per-sample-set results.
- **17e — `/evaluate <target>` REPL command.** Evaluates the most recent generation for a target; prints fitness leaderboard.
- **17f — Anti-cost safeguards.** Cap on calls per evaluation run; throttle.

### Phase 18 — GP Loop (autonomous)

**Goal:** Generations run as a background asyncio task. Throttled; user can pause/resume.

**Sub-phases:**

- **18a — Loop driver.** `evolution/loop.py`: asyncio task. On each tick: pick a target (round-robin or by `staleness`), generate a generation, evaluate, persist, sleep until next slot.
- **18b — Throttle / scheduler.** Honors `evolution.max_calls_per_hour`; backoff on errors; pauses on user-triggered work.
- **18c — Selection.** Top K variants survive; cross-generation lineage maintained. Recombination uses surviving variants as parents.
- **18d — `/evolution status` REPL command.** Active targets, generations completed today, current best fitness per target.
- **18e — `/evolution pause`, `/evolution resume`, `/evolution off`.**
- **18f — Cron support.** `evolution.cron` config; loop respects it.

## Acceptance Criteria for v0.1 Complete

You are done when all of the following are true:

1. CLI REPL responds; one-shot command runs and exits.
2. Manual `/architect`, `/operator`, `/casual` work and feel different.
3. `/auto` mode classifies intent and routes appropriately; manual override beats auto.
4. `UBONGO.md` edits change behavior across personas after `/reload`.
5. Conversation context persists across CLI restarts within a session; new session after 30 minutes silence.
6. Compaction triggers past threshold; summaries persisted and not regenerated.
7. Daily notes write to vault and render in Obsidian.
8. `summarize-conversation` skill works via `/summary`; bodies lazy-loaded.
9. `/reload` picks up persona/skill metadata edits without restart.
10. Every outbound message goes through `notification_queue`.
11. Master Agent classifies, plans, dispatches, governs, composes per turn; `/decisions` and `/trace` populated.
12. All eight worker agents (Research, Coding, Evaluator, Repair, Memory, Critic, Execution, Persona) registered and dispatchable; `/agents` lists them.
13. All six execution modes (sequential, parallel, competitive, collaborative, debate, speculative) selectable via `/mode` and produce distinguishable behavior.
14. Repair Agent recovers timeouts, parse errors, agent failures; rollbacks leave no partial state.
15. Decision matrix returns auto / ask_clarification / require_approval / reject per the `governance.yaml` rules; `governance_decisions` populated.
16. `require_approval` flow prompts user; y/n/why all work; Execution Agent properly sandboxed.
17. `/optimize <target>` generates variants; `/evaluate` produces a fitness leaderboard.
18. GP loop runs autonomously when enabled; throttled; pauseable.
19. `/improvements` lists pending promotions with diffs; approve/reject works; live-target swap takes effect; rollback works.
20. Semantic recall via `sqlite-vec` augments recency in `/recall`; vault-link graph queryable.
21. File watcher ingests vault edits; conflicts gated by approval flow.
22. Full `tests/manual/smoke_test.md` walkthrough passes end-to-end without manual fixup.
23. Total project size stays under ~15,000 lines of Python (excluding tests). If you're significantly over, the spec is doing too much; cut.

If the system survives a real day of use without manual intervention or crashes, ship.

## Out of Scope (v0.1)

Deferred deliberately. Don't sneak them in.

- Telegram channel and any other external transport: Slack, WhatsApp, Discord, web UI, voice. Telegram returns in v0.2.
- Notification policy engine, quiet hours, ad-hoc holds, hold-until-ack, catch-up summarizer. Deferred to v0.2 with Telegram.
- External integrations: Google Calendar, Gmail, Reddit, news. v0.2+, one at a time, each as a CLI script invoked through the constrained-bash skill.
- Multi-user support, RBAC, team features.
- Distributed deployment: Docker, Kubernetes, Temporal, Redis, NATS.
- Production observability dashboards (structured logs + `/audit` are enough).
- Web UI / mobile apps.
- Approval gates beyond text confirmation (e.g., signed-action receipts).
- Bidirectional sync with non-vault sources (Notion, etc.).

## Setup Instructions (for the README)

\```bash
# prerequisites
# - Python 3.11+
# - uv (https://docs.astral.sh/uv/)
# - An OpenRouter API key (https://openrouter.ai/)

git clone <repo>
cd ubongo
uv sync

# configure
cp .env.example .env
# edit .env with OPENROUTER_API_KEY
# edit config/UBONGO.md if you want to customize identity/preferences
# edit config/personas/*.md to tune voices

# run (REPL)
uv run python -m ubongo

# run (one-shot)
uv run python -m ubongo send "draft a migration plan"
\```

## Final Notes for Claude Code

- Build phase by phase. Don't start Phase N+1 before Phase N's testing plan and smoke test pass. Acceptance criteria are literal.
- Keep modules small. If a file exceeds 400 lines, split. The orchestrator is the exception (master.py may be larger).
- Write pytest tests for: classifier, master, runner, each agent, governance decision, evolution generator, evolution fitness, evolution lineage, memory store, memory compaction, memory embeddings, delivery queue, skills, events. Use the held-out sample fixture for evolution tests.
- No new dependencies beyond the tech-stack table without justification. In particular: no LangGraph, no Temporal, no `python-telegram-bot` until v0.2.
- The user's preferences apply to CLI output: prose over bullets, no em-dashes, no emojis, direct tone. These live in `config/UBONGO.md` and propagate via persona prompts.
- Every workflow goes through the queue. Every governance decision is persisted. Every agent run is traced. Every evolution variant has lineage. These are not optional.
- The Telegram channel in v0.2 should be additive: add `python-telegram-bot`, restore `allowed_user_ids` and the policy engine + quiet hours + holds, register the policy as a `before_send` handler. The router/agents/governance/evolution don't change.

8

Adapts persona and model to the kind of conversation you're having. Capabilities are organized as composable skills with progressive disclosure. A minimal SQLite-backed outbound queue sits on the response path so future proactive jobs (v0.3) have a delivery channel; the policy engine, quiet hours, and holds are deferred until Telegram lands in v0.2. Extension points are named events so v0.2+ behavior plugs in without rewriting the core loop.

This includes: Master Agent, worker agents (Research/Coding/Evaluator/Repair/Execution), parallel agent execution, debate mode, speculative execution, Genetic Programming, runtime self-modification, prompt evolution, sandboxing, embedding-based memory recall, semantic search, observability dashboards, approval gates beyond text confirmation, the notification policy engine, quiet hours, ad-hoc holds, hold-until-ack, catch-up summarization, and natural-language hold detection.
18	
19	## Core Design Decisions
20	
21	1. **Single channel: CLI.** v0.1 ships a REPL (`python -m ubongo`) and a one-shot command (`python -m ubongo send "..."`). Both share SQLite state. Telegram is the planned second channel after v0.1 works as desired.
22	2. **Router, not orchestrator.** A function that picks a persona, optionally one skill, and a model. No agent lifecycle, no spawning, no shutdown.
23	3. **Personas are configuration.** Markdown files containing system prompts. Personas define *voice*. Identity boilerplate is in a single global file (`UBONGO.md`), not duplicated across personas.
24	4. **Skills are configuration with progressive disclosure.** Folders with a `SKILL.md` and supporting prompts. Skills define *capabilities*. Only descriptions are loaded at startup; bodies load on activation. Personas and skills compose: any persona can run any skill.
25	5. **Hierarchical context loading.** System prompt for any turn is built by concatenating, in order: global context (`UBONGO.md`), active persona (`personas/<name>.md`), active skill body (if any). Closer-to-task layers come last.
26	6. **Memory: SQLite canonical, Markdown projected.** Conversation logs and structured facts live in SQLite. A daily notes Markdown file is generated for human inspection in Obsidian. Vault is write-only in v0.1.
27	7. **Compaction is a swappable function.** v0.1 ships a default (last N turns verbatim, prior history collapsed into a summary). The seam exists so v0.2 can swap in topic-aware or persona-specific strategies without touching memory storage.
28	8. **Models via OpenRouter.** Single API key, every model behind one interface, routed through LiteLLM. No Ollama in v0.1.
29	9. **All outbound messages flow through a minimal SQLite queue.** Even synchronous CLI responses pass through the queue (enqueued at `urgent`, dequeued and printed in the same turn). The queue persists so v0.3's scheduler has somewhere to write proactive output.
30	10. **Policy engine seam, no v0.1 logic.** The `before_send` event point is reserved for the policy engine. v0.1 ships a passthrough handler. The full engine — quiet hours, ad-hoc holds, hold-until-ack — lands when Telegram does in v0.2.
31	11. **Extension points are named events.** The bot's main loop emits events at well-defined points (`before_classify`, `after_classify`, `before_recall`, `after_recall`, `before_llm`, `after_llm`, `before_send`, `after_send`). v0.1 has only default handlers; v0.2+ adds handlers without modifying the core loop.
32	12. **Tool discipline.** v0.1 exposes zero tools to the LLM. When tools are added (v0.2+), prefer CLI scripts invoked via a single `bash` tool over first-class tool definitions. New first-class tools require justification.
33	13. **Configuration in YAML, secrets in `.env`.** Code reads config; config never contains secrets; secrets are loaded from environment at startup.
34	14. **One user.** The CLI runs locally; there is no auth boundary in v0.1. When Telegram lands in v0.2, the `allowed_user_ids` allowlist comes back.
